# Local development server commands
local_start() {
    print_header "Starting Local Development Server"

    # Check if Docker is running
    if ! docker info >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        print_error "Docker is not running. Please start Docker Desktop first."
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if services are already running
    if docker-compose ps | grep -q "Up"; then
        print_warning "Services are already running"
        docker-compose ps
        return 0
    fi

    print_status "Building and starting services..."
    docker-compose up -d --build

    print_status "Waiting for services to be ready..."
    sleep 5

    # Check health
    local attempts=0
    local max_attempts=10

    while [ $attempts -lt $max_attempts ]; do
        if curl -f -s http://localhost:$LOCAL_API_PORT/api/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            print_success "✅ Local server started successfully!"
            echo ""
            echo -e "${CYAN}Local URLs:${NC}"
            echo "  Web:    http://localhost:$LOCAL_WEB_PORT"
            echo "  API:    http://localhost:$LOCAL_API_PORT/api/health"
            echo "  Docs:   http://localhost:$LOCAL_API_PORT/api/openapi"
            echo ""
            print_status "View logs: $0 local:logs"
            return 0
        fi

        attempts=$((attempts + 1))
        sleep 2
    done

    print_error "Services started but not responding. Check logs with: $0 local:logs"
    docker-compose ps
    return 1
}

local_stop() {
    print_header "Stopping Local Development Server"

    if ! docker-compose ps | grep -q "Up"; then
        print_warning "No services are running"
        return 0
    fi

    docker-compose stop
    print_success "Local services stopped"
}

local_restart() {
    print_header "Restarting Local Development Server"

    docker-compose restart

    print_success "Services restarted"
    sleep 3
    local_status
}

local_status() {
    print_header "Local Service Status"

    if ! docker info >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        print_error "Docker is not running"
        return 1
    fi

    docker-compose ps
}

local_logs() {
    local service="${1:-}"

    print_header "Local Logs${service:+: $service}"

    if [ -z "$service" ]; then
        docker-compose logs --tail=50 -f
    else
        docker-compose logs --tail=50 -f "$service"
    fi
}

local_clean() {
    print_header "Cleaning Local Environment"

    print_warning "This will remove all containers, volumes, and data"
    read -p "Are you sure? (y/N) " -n 1 -r
    echo

    if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
        docker-compose down -v
        print_success "Local environment cleaned"
    else
        print_status "Operation cancelled"
    fi
}

local_dev() {
    print_header "Starting Local Development Mode"
    print_status "API will run on http://localhost:8083"
    print_status "Web will run on http://localhost:5173 (Vite dev server)"
    print_status "Changes will auto-reload. Press Ctrl+C to stop."
    echo ""

    # Kill existing processes on these ports
    print_status "Cleaning up existing processes..."
    lsof -ti:8083 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null || true
    lsof -ti:5173 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null || true

    # Create logs directory
    mkdir -p logs

    # Create trap to cleanup on exit
    trap 'print_warning "Stopping dev servers..."; kill $(jobs -p) 2>/dev/null; exit' INT TERM

    # Start API in background with Air for hot-reload
    print_status "Starting Go API with hot-reload (Air)..."
    cd api
    if ! command -v air &> /dev/null; then
        print_warning "Air not found. Installing..."
        go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest
    fi

    # Create Air config if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f .air.toml ]; then
        print_status "Creating Air config..."
        cat > .air.toml << 'EOF'
root = "."
testdata_dir = "testdata"
tmp_dir = "tmp"

[build]
args_bin = []
bin = "./tmp/main"
cmd = "go build -o ./tmp/main ./cmd/api"
delay = 1000
exclude_dir = ["assets", "tmp", "vendor", "testdata"]
exclude_file = []
exclude_regex = ["_test.go"]
exclude_unchanged = false
follow_symlink = false
full_bin = ""
include_dir = []
include_ext = ["go", "tpl", "tmpl", "html"]
include_file = []
kill_delay = "0s"
log = "build-errors.log"
poll = false
poll_interval = 0
post_cmd = []
pre_cmd = []
rerun = false
rerun_delay = 500
send_interrupt = false
stop_on_error = false

[color]
app = ""
build = "yellow"
main = "magenta"
runner = "green"
watcher = "cyan"

[log]
main_only = false
time = false

[misc]
clean_on_exit = false

[screen]
clear_on_rebuild = false
keep_scroll = true
EOF
    fi

    export DB_PATH="../data/taskai.db"
    export PORT=8083
    air > ../logs/api.log 2>&1 &
    API_PID=$!
    cd ..

    # Wait a bit for API to start
    sleep 2

    # Start Web dev server in background
    print_status "Starting Vite dev server with hot-reload..."
    cd web
    export VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:8083
    npm run dev > ../logs/web.log 2>&1 &
    WEB_PID=$!
    cd ..

    # Wait for servers to be ready
    sleep 3

    print_success "Development servers started!"
    echo ""
    print_success "✓ API: http://localhost:8083 (Go with Air hot-reload)"
    print_success "✓ Web: http://localhost:5173 (Vite hot-reload)"
    echo ""
    print_status "Logs:"
    print_status "  API: tail -f logs/api.log"
    print_status "  Web: tail -f logs/web.log"
    echo ""
    print_warning "Press Ctrl+C to stop both servers"
    echo ""

    # Tail both logs
    tail -f logs/api.log logs/web.log
}

# Main command handler
case ${1:-help} in
    local:start)
        local_start
        ;;
    local:stop)
        local_stop
        ;;
    local:restart)
        local_restart
        ;;
    local:status)
        local_status
        ;;
    local:logs)
        local_logs "$2"
        ;;
    local:clean)
        local_clean
        ;;
    deploy)
        shift
        deploy_to_staging "$*"
        ;;
    promote)
        promote_to_production "$2"
        ;;
    staging)
        case "$2" in
            status)
                env_status staging
                ;;
            logs)
                env_logs staging "$3"
                ;;
            health)
                env_health staging
                ;;
            restart)
                env_restart staging
                ;;
            stop)
                env_stop staging
                ;;
            start)
                env_start staging
                ;;
            admin)
                case "$3" in
                    create) admin_create "$4" "staging" ;;
                    list)   admin_list "staging" ;;
                    revoke) admin_revoke "$4" "staging" ;;
                    *)
                        print_error "Unknown admin command: $3"
                        echo "Available: create <email>, list, revoke <email>"
                        exit 1
                        ;;
                esac
                ;;
            *)
                print_error "Unknown staging command: $2"
                echo "Available: status, logs, health, restart, stop, start, admin"
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
        ;;
    prod)
        case "$2" in
            status)
                env_status production
                ;;
            logs)
                env_logs production "$3"
                ;;
            health)
                env_health production
                ;;
            restart)
                env_restart production
                ;;
            stop)
                env_stop production
                ;;
            start)
                env_start production
                ;;
            admin)
                case "$3" in
                    create) admin_create "$4" "production" ;;
                    list)   admin_list "production" ;;
                    revoke) admin_revoke "$4" "production" ;;
                    *)
                        print_error "Unknown admin command: $3"
                        echo "Available: create <email>, list, revoke <email>"
                        exit 1
                        ;;
                esac
                ;;
            *)
                print_error "Unknown production command: $2"
                echo "Available: status, logs, health, restart, stop, start, admin"
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
        ;;
    # Legacy shortcuts (default to staging for safety)
    restart)
        env_restart staging
        ;;
    stop)
        env_stop staging
        ;;
    start)
        env_start staging
        ;;
    status)
        env_status staging
        ;;
    logs)
        env_logs staging "$2"
        ;;
    health)
        env_health staging
        ;;
    test)
        run_all_tests
        ;;
    test-api)
        test_api
        ;;
    test-web)
        test_web
        ;;
    test-e2e)
        test_e2e
        ;;
    validate-openapi)
        validate_openapi
        ;;
    db-migrate)
        db_migrate "$2" "$3" "$4"
        ;;
    db-query)
        execute_db_query "$2"
        ;;
    admin)
        case "$2" in
            create)
                admin_create "$3" "remote"
                ;;
            list)
                admin_list "remote"
                ;;
            revoke)
                admin_revoke "$3" "remote"
                ;;
            *)
                print_error "Unknown admin command: $2"
                echo "Available: create <email>, list, revoke <email>"
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
        ;;
    local)
        case "$2" in
            dev)
                local_dev
                ;;
            admin)
                case "$3" in
                    create)
                        admin_create "$4" "local"
                        ;;
                    list)
                        admin_list "local"
                        ;;
                    revoke)
                        admin_revoke "$4" "local"
                        ;;
                    *)
                        print_error "Unknown local admin command: $3"
                        echo "Available: create <email>, list, revoke <email>"
                        exit 1
                        ;;
                esac
                ;;
            users:list)
                users_list_all
                ;;
            users:password)
                users_reset_password "$3" "$4"
                ;;
            *)
                print_error "Unknown local command: $2"
                echo "Available: admin, users:list, users:password"
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
        ;;
    remote)
        case "$2" in
            admin)
                case "$3" in
                    create)
                        admin_create "$4" "remote"
                        ;;
                    list)
                        admin_list "remote"
                        ;;
                    revoke)
                        admin_revoke "$4" "remote"
                        ;;
                    *)
                        print_error "Unknown remote admin command: $3"
                        echo "Available: create <email>, list, revoke <email>"
                        exit 1
                        ;;
                esac
                ;;
            *)
                print_error "Unknown remote command: $2"
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
        ;;
    help|--help|-h)
        show_help
        ;;
    *)
        print_error "Unknown command: $1"
        echo ""
        show_help
        exit 1
        ;;
esac

input.content:
# TaskAI Project Memory

## Infrastructure
- **Production**: `31.97.102.48` — `taskai.cc`, `mcp.taskai.cc`
- **Staging**: `129.213.82.37` — `staging.taskai.cc`, `mcp.staging.taskai.cc`
- Both accessible via `ssh ubuntu@<ip>`
- Webhook: `webhook.biswas.me` → staging server (129.213.82.37)
- Production webhook route: `taskai.cc/webhook/taskai` (nginx proxies to local :9876)

## Deployment
- Push to main → CI → staging deploy (via webhook on staging server)
- Promote: `gh workflow run deploy-production.yml -f ref="main"` (no `confirm` input)
- `./script/server deploy "message"` for quick staging deploys
- `./script/server promote` for production promotion
- `./script/server db-migrate prod staging` copies DB cross-server

## Docker Volumes (external)
- Production: `sprintspark_sprintspark-data` (named volume, external in compose as `prod-data`)
- Staging: `taskai-staging_taskai-staging-data` (new volume on staging server)
- DB file: `/data/taskai.db` inside volumes

## Key Gotchas
- Staging server is ARM64 (aarch64) — Docker builds may take longer
- Staging nginx is v1.24 — use `listen 443 ssl http2;` not `http2 on;` directive
- Cloudflare MCP (`mcp-remote`) is read-only (no DNS record creation)
- `api/api` binary is gitignored — don't track compiled binaries

    15→// Invite represents an invite record
    16→type Invite struct {
    17→	ID          int64   `json:"id"`

    19→	InviterID   int64   `json:"inviter_id"`
    20→	InviterName *string `json:"inviter_name,omitempty"`
    21→	InviteeID   *int64  `json:"invitee_id,omitempty"`
    22→	InviteeName *string `json:"invitee_name,omitempty"`
    23→	UsedAt      *string `json:"used_at,omitempty"`

    28→// InviteStatus is returned for invite validation

    35→// generateInviteCode creates a random URL-safe invite code
    36→func generateInviteCode() (string, error) {
    37→	b := make([]byte, 18)
    38→	if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
    39→		return "", err
    40→	}
    41→	return base64.URLEncoding.WithPadding(base64.NoPadding).EncodeToString(b), nil
    42→}

    44→// HandleListInvites returns the current user's invites
    45→func (s *Server) HandleListInvites(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    46→	userID, ok := GetUserID(r)
    47→	if !ok {
    48→		respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "user not authenticated", "unauthorized")
    49→		return
    50→	}
    51→
    52→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
    53→	defer cancel()
    54→
    55→	query := `
    56→		SELECT i.id, i.code, i.inviter_id, i.invitee_id, i.used_at, i.expires_at, i.created_at,
    57→			   u.name as invitee_name, u.email as invitee_email
    58→		FROM invites i
    59→		LEFT JOIN users u ON i.invitee_id = u.id
    60→		WHERE i.inviter_id = ?
    61→		ORDER BY i.created_at DESC
    62→	`
    63→
    64→	rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, query, userID)
    65→	if err != nil {
    66→		s.logger.Error("Failed to query invites", zap.Error(err))
    67→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to list invites", "internal_error")
    68→		return
    69→	}
    70→	defer rows.Close()
    71→
    72→	type InviteWithDetails struct {
    73→		ID          int64   `json:"id"`
    74→		Code        string  `json:"code"`
    75→		InviterID   int64   `json:"inviter_id"`
    76→		InviteeID   *int64  `json:"invitee_id,omitempty"`
    77→		InviteeName *string `json:"invitee_name,omitempty"`
    78→		UsedAt      *string `json:"used_at,omitempty"`
    79→		ExpiresAt   *string `json:"expires_at,omitempty"`
    80→		CreatedAt   string  `json:"created_at"`
    81→	}
    82→
    83→	invites := []InviteWithDetails{}
    84→	for rows.Next() {
    85→		var inv InviteWithDetails
    86→		var inviteeName, inviteeEmail sql.NullString
    87→		err := rows.Scan(&inv.ID, &inv.Code, &inv.InviterID, &inv.InviteeID, &inv.UsedAt, &inv.ExpiresAt, &inv.CreatedAt, &inviteeName, &inviteeEmail)
    88→		if err != nil {
    89→			s.logger.Error("Failed to scan invite row", zap.Error(err))
    90→			continue
    91→		}
    92→		if inviteeName.Valid && inviteeName.String != "" {
    93→			inv.InviteeName = &inviteeName.String
    94→		} else if inviteeEmail.Valid {
    95→			inv.InviteeName = &inviteeEmail.String
    96→		}
    97→		invites = append(invites, inv)
    98→	}
    99→
   100→	// Also get the user's invite count

   103→	err = s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT invite_count, is_admin FROM users WHERE id = ?`, userID).Scan(&inviteCount, &isAdmin)
   104→	if err != nil {
   105→		s.logger.Error("Failed to get invite count", zap.Error(err))
   106→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get invite count", "internal_error")
   107→		return
   108→	}
   109→
   110→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{
   111→		"invites":      invites,
   112→		"invite_count": inviteCount,
   113→		"is_admin":     isAdmin,
   114→	})

   117→// HandleCreateInvite creates a new invite code and optionally sends an email
   118→func (s *Server) HandleCreateInvite(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   119→	userID, ok := GetUserID(r)
   120→	if !ok {
   121→		respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "user not authenticated", "unauthorized")
   122→		return
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   126→	defer cancel()
   127→
   128→	// Parse optional email from request body
   129→	var req struct {
   130→		Email string `json:"email"`
   131→	}
   132→	// Body may be empty (backwards compatible)
   133→	_ = decodeJSON(r, &req)
   134→
   135→	// Check invite count (admins have unlimited)
   136→	var inviteCount int
   137→	var isAdmin bool
   138→	var inviterName string
   139→	err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT invite_count, is_admin, COALESCE(name, email) FROM users WHERE id = ?`, userID).Scan(&inviteCount, &isAdmin, &inviterName)
   140→	if err != nil {
   141→		s.logger.Error("Failed to get invite count", zap.Error(err))
   142→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create invite", "internal_error")
   143→		return
   144→	}
   145→
   146→	if !isAdmin && inviteCount <= 0 {
   147→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "no invites remaining", "no_invites")
   148→		return
   149→	}
   150→
   151→	// Generate invite code
   152→	code, err := generateInviteCode()
   153→	if err != nil {
   154→		s.logger.Error("Failed to generate invite code", zap.Error(err))
   155→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create invite", "internal_error")
   156→		return
   157→	}
   158→
   159→	// Set expiry to 7 days
   160→	expiresAt := time.Now().Add(7 * 24 * time.Hour)
   161→
   162→	tx, err := s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
   163→	if err != nil {
   164→		s.logger.Error("Failed to begin transaction", zap.Error(err))
   165→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create invite", "internal_error")
   166→		return
   167→	}
   168→	defer tx.Rollback()
   169→
   170→	// Insert invite
   171→	_, err = tx.ExecContext(ctx,
   172→		`INSERT INTO invites (code, inviter_id, expires_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)`,
   173→		code, userID, expiresAt,
   174→	)
   175→	if err != nil {
   176→		s.logger.Error("Failed to insert invite", zap.Error(err))
   177→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create invite", "internal_error")
   178→		return
   179→	}
   180→
   181→	// Decrement invite count (only for non-admins)
   182→	if !isAdmin {
   183→		_, err = tx.ExecContext(ctx,
   184→			`UPDATE users SET invite_count = invite_count - 1 WHERE id = ? AND invite_count > 0`,
   185→			userID,
   186→		)
   187→		if err != nil {
   188→			s.logger.Error("Failed to decrement invite count", zap.Error(err))
   189→			respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create invite", "internal_error")
   190→			return
   191→		}
   192→	}
   193→
   194→	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
   195→		s.logger.Error("Failed to commit transaction", zap.Error(err))
   196→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create invite", "internal_error")
   197→		return
   198→	}
   199→
   200→	s.logger.Info("Invite created", zap.Int64("user_id", userID), zap.String("code", code[:8]+"..."))
   201→
   202→	// Send email if requested and email service is available
   203→	emailSent := false
   204→	if req.Email != "" {
   205→		if emailSvc := s.GetEmailService(); emailSvc != nil {
   206→			appURL := s.getAppURL()
   207→			if err := emailSvc.SendUserInvite(ctx, req.Email, inviterName, code, appURL); err != nil {
   208→				s.logger.Warn("Failed to send invite email",
   209→					zap.String("to", req.Email),
   210→					zap.Error(err),
   211→				)
   212→			} else {
   213→				emailSent = true
   214→			}
   215→		}
   216→	}
   217→
   218→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, map[string]interface{}{
   219→		"code":       code,
   220→		"expires_at": expiresAt.Format(time.RFC3339),
   221→		"email_sent": emailSent,
   222→	})
   223→}
   224→
   225→// HandleValidateInvite checks if an invite code is valid (public endpoint)
   226→func (s *Server) HandleValidateInvite(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   227→	code := r.URL.Query().Get("code")
   228→	if code == "" {
   229→		respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, InviteStatus{Valid: false, Message: "invite code is required"})
   230→		return
   231→	}
   232→
   233→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   234→	defer cancel()
   235→
   236→	var inviterName sql.NullString
   237→	var usedAt sql.NullString
   238→	var expiresAt sql.NullString
   239→	err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
   240→		`SELECT u.name, u.email, i.used_at, i.expires_at
   241→		 FROM invites i JOIN users u ON i.inviter_id = u.id
   242→		 WHERE i.code = ?`, code,
   243→	).Scan(&inviterName, new(string), &usedAt, &expiresAt)
   244→
   245→	if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   246→		respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, InviteStatus{Valid: false, Message: "invalid invite code"})
   247→		return
   248→	}
   249→	if err != nil {
   250→		s.logger.Error("Failed to validate invite", zap.Error(err))
   251→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to validate invite", "internal_error")
   252→		return
   253→	}
   254→
   255→	if usedAt.Valid {
   256→		respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, InviteStatus{Valid: false, Message: "this invite has already been used"})
   257→		return
   258→	}
   259→
   260→	if expiresAt.Valid {
   261→		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, expiresAt.String)
   262→		if err == nil && time.Now().After(t) {
   263→			respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, InviteStatus{Valid: false, Message: "this invite has expired"})
   264→			return
   265→		}
   266→		// Also try parsing as the SQLite default format
   267→		t2, err2 := time.Parse("2006-01-02 15:04:05-07:00", expiresAt.String)
   268→		if err2 == nil && time.Now().After(t2) {
   269→			respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, InviteStatus{Valid: false, Message: "this invite has expired"})
   270→			return
   271→		}
   272→	}
   273→
   274→	name := ""
   275→	if inviterName.Valid && inviterName.String != "" {
   276→		name = inviterName.String
   277→	}
   278→
   279→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, InviteStatus{Valid: true, InviterName: name})
   280→}
   281→
   282→// HandleAdminBoostInvites allows admins to set a user's invite count
   283→func (s *Server) HandleAdminBoostInvites(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   284→	userID, ok := GetUserID(r)
   285→	if !ok {
   286→		respondError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "user not authenticated", "unauthorized")
   287→		return
   288→	}
   289→
   290→	if !s.isAdmin(r.Context(), userID) {
   291→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "admin access required", "forbidden")
   292→		return
   293→	}
   294→
   295→	targetUserIDStr := r.PathValue("id")
   296→	if targetUserIDStr == "" {
   297→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "user id required", "validation_error")
   298→		return
   299→	}
   300→
   301→	var targetUserID int64
   302→	if _, err := fmt.Sscanf(targetUserIDStr, "%d", &targetUserID); err != nil {
   303→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid user id", "validation_error")
   304→		return
   305→	}
   306→
   307→	var req struct {
   308→		InviteCount int `json:"invite_count"`
   309→	}
   310→	if err := decodeJSON(r, &req); err != nil {
   311→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid request body", "invalid_request")
   312→		return
   313→	}
   314→
   315→	if req.InviteCount < 0 {
   316→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invite count must be non-negative", "validation_error")
   317→		return
   318→	}
   319→
   320→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   321→	defer cancel()
   322→
   323→	result, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `UPDATE users SET invite_count = ? WHERE id = ?`, req.InviteCount, targetUserID)
   324→	if err != nil {
   325→		s.logger.Error("Failed to update invite count", zap.Error(err))
   326→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to update invite count", "internal_error")
   327→		return
   328→	}
   329→
   330→	rowsAffected, _ := result.RowsAffected()
   331→	if rowsAffected == 0 {
   332→		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "user not found", "not_found")
   333→		return
   334→	}
   335→
   336→	s.logger.Info("Admin boosted invites",
   337→		zap.Int64("admin_id", userID),
   338→		zap.Int64("target_user_id", targetUserID),
   339→		zap.Int("invite_count", req.InviteCount),
   340→	)
   341→
   342→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{
   343→		"id":           targetUserID,
   344→		"invite_count": req.InviteCount,
   345→	})
   346→}
   347→

file.content:
name: Deploy to Staging

on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI"]
    types: [completed]
    branches: [main]

  # Allow manual trigger from GitHub UI
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    environment: staging
    if: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}

    steps:
      - name: Trigger staging webhook
        run: |
          echo "=== Deploying TaskAI to STAGING ==="
          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST "https://webhook.biswas.me/webhook/taskai-staging" \
            -H "X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=$(echo -n '{}' | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac '${{ secrets.STAGING_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}' | awk '{print $2}')" \
            -H "X-GitHub-Event: push" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{}')
          echo "Webhook response: $RESPONSE"
          if [ "$RESPONSE" -lt 200 ] || [ "$RESPONSE" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "WARNING: Webhook returned $RESPONSE, deployment may not have triggered"
          fi

      - name: Wait for deployment
        run: |
          echo "Waiting 60 seconds for deployment to complete..."
          sleep 60

      - name: Verify staging health
        run: |
          echo "-> Checking API health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf https://staging.taskai.cc/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: API is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking web health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf -o /dev/null https://staging.taskai.cc/ 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: Web is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking MCP health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 10); do
            if curl -sf https://mcp.staging.taskai.cc/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: MCP is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 10 ]; then
              echo "WARNING: MCP health check failed (non-blocking)"
            fi
            sleep 3
          done

          echo ""
          echo "=== Staging deployment verified! ==="
          echo "URL: https://staging.taskai.cc"

content.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
APP_DIR="/home/ubuntu/taskai-staging"
cd "$APP_DIR"
echo "=== Deploying TaskAI (staging) ==="
echo "-> Building Docker images..."
docker compose build --no-cache
echo "-> Stopping old containers..."
docker compose down
echo "-> Starting new containers..."
docker compose up -d
echo "-> Waiting for services to be healthy..."
sleep 10
echo "-> Checking API health..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8082/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: API is healthy"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
        docker compose logs api | tail -20
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
done
echo "-> Checking web health..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8086/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Web is healthy"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
        docker compose logs web | tail -20
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
done
docker image prune -f
echo "=== Deployment completed! ==="
echo "URL: https://staging.taskai.cc"

content.thinking:
Now I can see the issue. The last taskai-staging deployment attempt at `2026-02-18 02:32:30` shows:

```
[taskai-staging] - WARNING - Repository mismatch: got None, expected anchoo2kewl/taskai
[taskai-staging] - WARNING - Background deployment issue: Repository mismatch
```

The webhook is receiving a request with no repository info (it's sending an empty `{}` body). The GitHub Actions workflow sends `'{}'` as the body, which doesn't include the repository info. The webhook server is checking for the repository name in the payload and rejecting it because it's `None` instead of `anchoo2kewl/taskai`.


The staging deploy workflow is sending an empty payload when the webhook server expects a proper GitHub webhook format with repository details. The GitHub Actions logs show success because the webhook returns a 200, but the actual deployment gets rejected server-side. I need to update the deploy-staging.yml to include the proper repository information in the payload, and I should check if the production webhook has the same problem.

content.text:
Found the issue! The webhook log shows:

```
[taskai-staging] - WARNING - Repository mismatch: got None, expected anchoo2kewl/taskai
```

The deploy workflow sends an empty `{}` body, which has no repository info. The webhook server expects a `repository.full_name` field in the payload. Let me fix both deploy workflows.

file.content:
name: Deploy to Staging

on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI"]
    types: [completed]
    branches: [main]

  # Allow manual trigger from GitHub UI
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    environment: staging
    if: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}

    steps:
      - name: Trigger staging webhook
        run: |
          echo "=== Deploying TaskAI to STAGING ==="
          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST "https://webhook.biswas.me/webhook/taskai-staging" \
            -H "X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=$(echo -n '{}' | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac '${{ secrets.STAGING_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}' | awk '{print $2}')" \
            -H "X-GitHub-Event: push" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{}')
          echo "Webhook response: $RESPONSE"
          if [ "$RESPONSE" -lt 200 ] || [ "$RESPONSE" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "WARNING: Webhook returned $RESPONSE, deployment may not have triggered"
          fi

      - name: Wait for deployment
        run: |
          echo "Waiting 60 seconds for deployment to complete..."
          sleep 60

      - name: Verify staging health
        run: |
          echo "-> Checking API health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf https://staging.taskai.cc/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: API is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking web health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf -o /dev/null https://staging.taskai.cc/ 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: Web is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking MCP health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 10); do
            if curl -sf https://mcp.staging.taskai.cc/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: MCP is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 10 ]; then
              echo "WARNING: MCP health check failed (non-blocking)"
            fi
            sleep 3
          done

          echo ""
          echo "=== Staging deployment verified! ==="
          echo "URL: https://staging.taskai.cc"

name: Deploy to Production

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ref:
        description: 'Git ref to deploy'
        required: false
        default: 'main'

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    environment: production

    steps:
      - name: Display deployment info
        run: |
          echo "Deploying to PRODUCTION"
          echo "Ref: ${{ github.event.inputs.ref }}"
          echo "Triggered by: ${{ github.actor }}"

      - name: Trigger production webhook
        run: |
          echo "=== Deploying TaskAI to PRODUCTION ==="
          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST "https://taskai.cc/webhook/taskai" \
            -H "X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=$(echo -n '{}' | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac '${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}' | awk '{print $2}')" \
            -H "X-GitHub-Event: push" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{}')
          echo "Webhook response: $RESPONSE"
          if [ "$RESPONSE" -lt 200 ] || [ "$RESPONSE" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "WARNING: Webhook returned $RESPONSE, deployment may not have triggered"
          fi

      - name: Wait for deployment
        run: |
          echo "Waiting 60 seconds for deployment to complete..."
          sleep 60

      - name: Verify production health
        run: |
          echo "-> Checking API health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf https://taskai.cc/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: API is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking web health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf -o /dev/null https://taskai.cc/ 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: Web is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking MCP health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 10); do
            if curl -sf https://mcp.taskai.cc/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: MCP is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 10 ]; then
              echo "WARNING: MCP health check failed (non-blocking)"
            fi
            sleep 3
          done

          echo ""
          echo "=== Production deployment completed! ==="
          echo "URL: https://taskai.cc"

content.text: Both workflows send `'{}'` as the body, which has no `repository` field. The webhook server rejects it with "Repository mismatch: got None, expected anchoo2kewl/taskai". I need to send a proper payload with repository info. The HMAC signature also needs to match the new payload.

- name: Trigger staging webhook
        run: |
          echo "=== Deploying TaskAI to STAGING ==="
          PAYLOAD='{"repository":{"full_name":"anchoo2kewl/taskai"},"ref":"refs/heads/main"}'
          SIGNATURE=$(echo -n "$PAYLOAD" | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac '${{ secrets.STAGING_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}' | awk '{print $2}')
          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST "https://webhook.biswas.me/webhook/taskai-staging" \
            -H "X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=$SIGNATURE" \
            -H "X-GitHub-Event: push" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "$PAYLOAD")
          echo "Webhook response: $RESPONSE"
          if [ "$RESPONSE" -lt 200 ] || [ "$RESPONSE" -ge 300 ]; then

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Deploy to Staging

on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI"]
    types: [completed]
    branches: [main]

  # Allow manual trigger from GitHub UI
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    environment: staging
    if: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}

    steps:
      - name: Trigger staging webhook
        run: |
          echo "=== Deploying TaskAI to STAGING ==="
          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST "https://webhook.biswas.me/webhook/taskai-staging" \
            -H "X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=$(echo -n '{}' | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac '${{ secrets.STAGING_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}' | awk '{print $2}')" \
            -H "X-GitHub-Event: push" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{}')
          echo "Webhook response: $RESPONSE"
          if [ "$RESPONSE" -lt 200 ] || [ "$RESPONSE" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "WARNING: Webhook returned $RESPONSE, deployment may not have triggered"
          fi

      - name: Wait for deployment
        run: |
          echo "Waiting 60 seconds for deployment to complete..."
          sleep 60

      - name: Verify staging health
        run: |
          echo "-> Checking API health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf https://staging.taskai.cc/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: API is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking web health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf -o /dev/null https://staging.taskai.cc/ 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: Web is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking MCP health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 10); do
            if curl -sf https://mcp.staging.taskai.cc/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: MCP is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 10 ]; then
              echo "WARNING: MCP health check failed (non-blocking)"
            fi
            sleep 3
          done

          echo ""
          echo "=== Staging deployment verified! ==="
          echo "URL: https://staging.taskai.cc"

name: Deploy to Production

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ref:
        description: 'Git ref to deploy'
        required: false
        default: 'main'

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    environment: production

    steps:
      - name: Display deployment info
        run: |
          echo "Deploying to PRODUCTION"
          echo "Ref: ${{ github.event.inputs.ref }}"
          echo "Triggered by: ${{ github.actor }}"

      - name: Trigger production webhook
        run: |
          echo "=== Deploying TaskAI to PRODUCTION ==="
          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST "https://taskai.cc/webhook/taskai" \
            -H "X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=$(echo -n '{}' | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac '${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}' | awk '{print $2}')" \
            -H "X-GitHub-Event: push" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{}')
          echo "Webhook response: $RESPONSE"
          if [ "$RESPONSE" -lt 200 ] || [ "$RESPONSE" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "WARNING: Webhook returned $RESPONSE, deployment may not have triggered"
          fi

      - name: Wait for deployment
        run: |
          echo "Waiting 60 seconds for deployment to complete..."
          sleep 60

      - name: Verify production health
        run: |
          echo "-> Checking API health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf https://taskai.cc/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: API is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking web health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf -o /dev/null https://taskai.cc/ 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: Web is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking MCP health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 10); do
            if curl -sf https://mcp.taskai.cc/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: MCP is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 10 ]; then
              echo "WARNING: MCP health check failed (non-blocking)"
            fi
            sleep 3
          done

          echo ""
          echo "=== Production deployment completed! ==="
          echo "URL: https://taskai.cc"

content.content: 87790a9 fix(ci): include repository payload in deploy webhook requests

toolUseResult.stdout: 87790a9 fix(ci): include repository payload in deploy webhook requests

content.text:
Staging is healthy and running commit `87790a9` which includes both the admin invite fix and the webhook payload fix.

**Summary of what happened:**
1. The deploy webhook payloads were empty `{}`, causing the webhook server to reject them with "Repository mismatch: got None" - this meant **no deployments were actually happening** since the server migration, they just appeared successful in GitHub Actions because the webhook returned 200 before processing.
2. Fixed both `deploy-staging.yml` and `deploy-production.yml` to send `{"repository":{"full_name":"anchoo2kewl/taskai"},"ref":"refs/heads/main"}` with a correctly computed HMAC signature.
3. Staging is now deployed with all changes including the admin invite fix (∞ for admins).

The admin invite fix should now be visible on `staging.taskai.cc`. Try refreshing with a hard reload (Cmd+Shift+R) to bypass any browser cache.
